rules.name: comment-why-not-what
rules.evidence: **NEVER** comment the WHAT of code (the code itself shows what it does) / **ONLY** add comments to explain WHY for unusual, unclear, or non-obvious implementations

rules.name: beginblocker-endblocker-no-panic
rules.evidence: BeginBlocker and Endblocker functions in module should never panic, it would cause a chain halt

rules.name: golangci-lint-scope
rules.evidence: `golangci-lint run` - Only if you modified Go code

rules.name: gofumpt-scope
rules.evidence: `gofumpt -w .` - Only if you modified Go code

rules.name: proto-gen-format-scope
rules.evidence: `make proto-format` and `make proto-gen` - Only if you modified .proto files

rules.name: go-test-scope
rules.evidence: `go test` - Only for packages you modified

rules.name: go-acc-full-suite-scope
rules.evidence: Full test suite with `go-acc` - Only before final push or if requested

uncompilable: Cosmos SDK code runs single-threaded (no multithreading) — factual statement, not tied to an action
Transactions are atomic and rollback on error/panic — factual statement, not tied to an action
All cosmos validators must get the same result so code should be deterministic — general design principle, no specific pattern to bind to an action

Security Best Practices list for dymd CLI usage (never share keys, use hardware wallets, verify tx before signing, etc.) — end-user operational guidance for interacting with the chain, not a Claude Code coding action
